title: Functions 2
description: Functions can take parameters. In Rust, you must declare the type of each parameter.
instructions: Fix the function signature by adding the correct parameter type.
hints: Function parameters in Rust must have a type annotation.
The parameter is used in a range (0..num), so it should be an integer type.
Try using `i32` or `u32` as the type for the parameter.
explanation: In Rust function signatures, you must declare the type of each parameter. The syntax is `name: Type`. This is a deliberate design choice - the compiler can almost always infer types within function bodies, but requiring type annotations on function boundaries makes code more readable and provides clear documentation.
